channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Ratajski beats Wattimena to lift European Darts Open title
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Krzysztof Ratajski defeated Jermaine Wattimena 8-6 in a nail-biting final to win the European Darts Open in Leverkusen.

item.title: Papers: Arsenal turn to Alvarez with Barcola unavailable
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Monday's newspapers...

item.title: Massive Bangkok pub fire kills at least 27 people, Thai officials say
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 27 people were killed and 63 injured after a fire tore through a Bangkok bar late on Sunday, Thai officials said. Bangkok Governor Chadchart Sittipunt said the blaze spread rapidly, with smoke believed to be the main cause of death. Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause.

item.title: Bellingham and Tuchel's ‘battle of wills’ is healthy for England
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Passionate, driven, uncompromising. Those are terms that can be equally applied to both Thomas Tuchel and Jude Bellingham.

item.title: ADC Chairman David Mark Urges Graduates to Embrace Integrity, Innovation, Lifelong Learning
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Chairman of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), Senator David Mark, has urged graduating students of Pace Setters College, Abuja, to embrace integrity, hard work and lifelong learning as they prepare to face the challenges of a rapidly changing world. Speaking on Sunday as the guest

item.title: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Oil Prices Rise Amid New U.S.-Iran Attacks; Nvidia, Micron, Sandisk Near Buy Points
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Alavin Withdraws Claims Against Wizkid’s Associate Godson
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Emmanuel Jakpa, popularly known as Alavin, has withdrawn allegations contained in a viral video against businessman and social media influencer Uyi Godson Agho, popularly known as Godson, urging members of the public to disregard the recording. The retraction comes days after Godson, an associate of

item.title: Shettima’s Retention Signals APC’s Strength In North, Youth Parliament Declares
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The APC Youth Parliament has expressed confidence that President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s decision to retain vice president Kashim Shettima as his running mate for the 2027 general elections will strengthen the ruling party’s chances of victory, particularly in the North-East and the Northern

item.title: Nestlé Nigeria Reiterates Commitment to Creating Enabling Environments for Youth Entrepreneurship
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Abuja, Nigeria – Nestlé Nigeria and Activate Success International Foundation (ASIF) reaffirm their existing eight-year partnership to advance youth entrepreneurship and empowerment across the country. Building on a robust history

item.title: MTN Nigeria Unveils Bundled Broadband, Router Plans to Boost Internet Access
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: MTN Nigeria has introduced bundled broadband solutions that combine data plans with compatible routers as part of efforts to expand broadband penetration and improve internet access for homes, small businesses

item.title: Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of strikes between U.S. and Iran
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices rose and U.S. stock-index futures slipped on Sunday, after the U.S. and Iran continued their tit-for-tat attacks around the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend.

item.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Iranian forces earlier said the Strait was closed and launched wide-ranging attacks at US allies and bases in the region.

item.title: Uruguay announce Diego Forlan as new coach
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Uruguay has appointed legendary former striker Diego Forlan as interim head coach of the national team following Marcelo Bielsa's resignation after La Celeste's disappointing group-stage exit at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post Uruguay announce Diego Forlan as new coach appeared first on Vanguard N

item.title: Oil Climbs, US Futures Dip on Fresh Iran Strikes: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil jumped and US equity futures fell after the US launched another round of strikes against Iran, while conflicting claims over the status of the Strait of Hormuz heightened uncertainty.

item.title: Abbas hails Shettima’s APC re-nomination
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: He also commended President Tinubu for retaining confidence in Shettima, saying the decision reflected strong leadership and reinforced unity within the ruling APC. The post Abbas hails Shettima’s APC re-nomination appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Donald Trump pays tribute to the 71-year-old South Carolina senator, calling him a "true American patriot" who will be "greatly missed".

item.title: Oil Jumps as US and Iran Trade Strikes, Dispute Hormuz Status
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil jumped as the US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes, with the sides offering conflicting statements on whether the Strait of Hormuz remains open to shipping.

item.title: Tinubu Has Given NIMC Until End Of 2026 To Enrol Every Nigerian, Says DG
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: She stressed that the National Identification Number (NIN) remains a unique identifier, ensuring that every individual is registered only once. The post Tinubu Has Given NIMC Until End Of 2026 To Enrol Every Nigerian, Says DG appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Detained by settlers, US Democrat Ro Khanna now faces pro-Israel attacks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Progressive congressman accuses Israeli military of 'lying' after settlers held up his van in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: World No. 1 defends Wimbledon title
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Jannik Sinner is Wimbledon champion once again! The world No. 1 defeated Alexander Zverev 6-7, 7-6, 6-3, 6-4 in a thrilling four-set final to defend his title. The turning point came when Zverev slipped and appeared to injure his knee in the third set, allowing Sinner to seize control. The Italian k

item.title: Arsenal want Argentina's Alvarez - Monday's gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Arsenal want to sign Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez, Aston Villa hijack Newcastle's move for Johan Manzambi, plus more.

item.title: Telecom Rally Adds N5.67tn To MTN, Airtel On Earnings Optimism
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Nigeria’s two largest listed telecommunications companies, MTN Nigeria Plc and Airtel Africa Plc, added a combined N5.67tn to their market capitalisation within the first 10 trading days of the second half of 2026, defying a broad sell-off on the Nigerian Exchange Limited (NGX) that erased gai

item.title: Ex-Defence Spokesman Enenche Hails Rescue Of Oyo Abductees

item.description: The former defence spokesman's comment came days after the presidency and military authorities announced the rescue of the abductees. The post Ex-Defence Spokesman Enenche Hails Rescue Of Oyo Abductees appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Tantita’s Oil Asset Protection Key To Nigeria’s $1tn Economy Target
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Achieving the Federal Government’s vision of growing Nigeria into a $1tn economy by 2030 will not be a walk in the park. It requires a stable and prosperous economy driven by peace and security. The peace and stability of the Niger Delta, coupled with the protection of critical national assets

item.title: Israel releases video of huge detonations in Lebanon
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The Israeli military has released video showing huge explosions and detonations in villages in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Tanzania’s richest man offers $100m for Dangote’s planned Kenya refinery

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: They described kidnapping as a crime with “no place in civilized communities” and said it was targeted at innocent children seeking education

item.title: Nigeria pumps above OPEC quota as output hits six-year- high
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Nigeria has again surpassed its OPEC-mandated production ceiling for the second consecutive month, with June crude oil output soaring to read more Nigeria pumps above OPEC quota as output hits six-year- high

item.title: Ukraine PM exits as Zelensky effects wartime cabinet change
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Prime Minister of Ukraine, Y​uliia Svyrydenko, has stepped down from the position after one year in office, President Volodymyr Zelensky announced on Sunday. Svyrydenko​ became the 19th Prime Minister ​on July 17, 2025, after being approved by the ​parliament. She previously served as First Depu

item.title: Police arrest man for allegedly beating wife to death in Nasarawa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to the suspect's confession, he acted out of anger after the deceased allegedly refused to prepare food, following an argument that ensued, which escalated into a physical altercation that ultimately resulted in her death; however, he expressed remorse over his actions. item.title: Transfer: Trossard agrees deal to leave Arsenal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Arsenal forward, Leandro Trossard, is close to agreeing personal terms with Besiktas ahead of a summer move. According to The Athletic, the agreement between both clubs is in it final stages. Besiktas will pay the Premier League champions a fee around €18m upfront and €2m in add-ons. Trossard is exp

item.title: Transfer: Portuguese club SC Braga sign Nigerian youngster
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Portuguese Primeira Liga club SC Braga have completed the signing of Nigerian defender Adebisi Olamikun from Inter Lagos, DAILY POST reports. The Minho Warriors beat competition from other top clubs in Europe to the signature of the young centre-back. Olamikun signed a long-term contract with SC Bra

item.title: Deadly Israeli strikes pound Gaza
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Israel’s military has attacked several places across Gaza.

item.title: ‘I don’t need to be presidential candidate to educate you’ – Umahi hits back at Peter Obi

item.description: Nigeria’s Minister of Works, Dave Umahi, has fired back at the presidential candidate of the Nigerian Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, saying he does not need to be a presidential candidate to educate the former Anambra State governor on infrastructure. Addressing journalists at a constru

item.title: Lindsey Graham likely died from aortic rupture, medical examiner says
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US Senator Lindsey Graham, a close ally of President Donald Trump and a leading Republican voice on foreign policy, died on Saturday after a brief illness, his office said. He was 71. The District of Columbia medical examiner said the preliminary cause of death was an aortic dissection linked to art

item.title: Nigerian army foils kidnap attempt, rescues four victims in Sokoto
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army’s Operation FANSAN YAMMA have successfully thwarted a kidnapping attempt by suspected terrorists in Surimi, in Danko-Wasagu Local Government Area of Kebbi State, which borders Sokoto State. The army said that the troops rescued four individuals who had been kidnappe

item.description: France defender Ibrahima Konate has brushed aside Lamine Yamal's bold pre-match comments as the reigning European champions prepare to face Spain in the 2026 FIFA World Cup semi-final on Tuesday. item.title: Israel to hold national elections on October 27, parliament says
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Netanyahu, Israel's longest-serving prime minister, has confirmed that he will run for office again.

item.title: Argentina Legend Antonio Rattín Dies At 89 Ahead Of England World Cup Showdown
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Argentina football legend Antonio Rattín, the former national team captain famously sent off during the 1966 FIFA World Cup quarter-final against England, has died at the age of 89. Rattín’s death was confirmed by his lifelong club Boca Juniors on Saturday, coinciding with Argentina and England both

item.title: Sinner shatters Zverev’s Wimbledon dream, retains crown
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: World number one Jannik Sinner fought back from a set down to defeat Alexander Zverev and successfully defend his Wimbledon title on Sunday.

item.title: US Says It Began Launching More Strikes Against Iran at 5pm ET
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: US Central Command forces began launching more strikes against Iran at 5 p.m. ET today, to continue degrading their ability to attack civilian mariners and commercial ships freely transiting the Strait of Hormuz, according to a post on X.

item.title: Protest on London’s Tower Bridge demands release of Dr Hussam Abu Safia
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Pro-Palestine activists unfurled a giant Palestinian flag and a banner on London's Tower Bridge.

item.title: Lindsey Graham, hardline backer of US-Israeli wars, dies aged 71
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of Israel and one of Washington’s most prominent foreign policy hawks, has died.

He stated that the development was in continuation of sustained operations against criminal activiti

The defender left Bayelsa United following the club’s relegation from the NPFL. Williams featured regularly for the P

item.title: Irish datacenters now guzzle 23% of the country's electricity
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.theregister.com/on-prem/2026/07/11/irish-datacenters-now-guzzle-23-of-the-countrys-electricity/5270013 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48884322 Points: 131 # Comments: 81

item.title: UN Envoy, Ibrahim Seeks Global Debt-For-Education Swap At UNESCO Conference
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Jimoh Ibrahim , has called for a bold global debt-for-education swap initiative to enable developing countries invest more in education without undermining their debt obligations. The post UN Envoy, Ibrahim Seeks Global Debt-For-Ed

item.title: INEC Portal: APC mocks opposition over deadline extension for uploading candidates’ names
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The APC says that though INEC acted within its statutory powers and administrative discretion, the circumstances that led to the extension exposed the organisational shortcomings of the opposition parties and their capacity to govern. The post INEC Portal: APC mocks opposition over deadline extensi

item.title: Qatar’s former emir remembered as ‘a man of vision and courage’
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US diplomat Adam Ereli remembers Qatar’s former Emir Sheikh as a personable and charismatic leader.

item.title: At least 27 killed in Bangkok bar fire
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through blasting flames to escape.

item.title: FG raids illegal gold camp in Osun, arrests two suspects
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Officials recovered two motorcycles, pumping machines, Lister generators, mineral washing equipment, long hose pipes, gold-trapping carpets, cutlasses and other tools believed to have been used in the illegal mining activities. The operational camp was dismantled and the site sealed in line with the

item.title: Lorde says Ray-Ban Meta AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Lorde was performing at the Real Cool Festival in Madrid on Thursday and took some time during her set to speak out against AI glasses. While she didn't specify any brands in particular, it's likely she was taking a shot at festival sponsor Ray-Ban, which has collaborated with Meta on a pair of AI s

item.title: Man Arrested For Allegedly Beating Wife To Death Over Food In Nasarawa
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The suspect was arrested and allegedly confessed during interrogation, claiming he attacked his wife in anger after she reportedly refused to prepare food. The post Man Arrested For Allegedly Beating Wife To Death Over Food In Nasarawa appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: ‘Exceptional individuals like Soyinka do not come often in generation’ – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has paid tribute to Nobel laureate Prof. Wole Soyinka on his 92nd birthday, describing him as an exceptional figure whose contributions to literature, democracy and good governance have left a lasting impact on Nigeria and the world. In a birthday message released on Sunday ahe

item.title: Defamation: EFCC appeals against judgement awarding N10m damages to ex-minister
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: EFCC also applied for a stay of execution to halt the enforcement of judgement pending the determination of its appeal. The post Defamation: EFCC appeals against judgement awarding N10m damages to ex-minister appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.
